blurb: Multidisciplinary research department at the University of Oxford studying the social, economic and political implications of the internet and AI.

publications.title: Training language models to be warm can reduce accuracy and increase sycophancy
publications.byline: Ibrahim, L., Hafner, F.S. and Rocher, L. (2026)
publications.summary: Nature paper (vol 652, 2026) finding that optimising LLMs for warmth can trade off against accuracy and increase sycophancy.

publications.title: Reliability of LLMs as medical assistants for the general public: a randomized preregistered study
publications.byline: Bean, A.M., Payne, R.E., Parsons, G., Kirk, H.R. et al. (2026)
publications.summary: Nature Medicine (vol 32, 2026) randomized preregistered study assessing reliability of large language models as medical assistants for general public use.

publications.title: A Framework for Evaluating Global AI Governance Initiatives
publications.byline: Roberts, H., Taddeo, M. and Floridi, L. (2026)
publications.summary: Global Policy article (2026) proposing a framework to assess and compare international AI governance initiatives.

news.title: Rethinking EU AI policy: why public subsidies for AI should deliver real wellbeing
news.summary: A new OII working paper argues EU technology policy should hold AI companies accountable for actual public wellbeing, not just reduced risk — a values-based reframing of how the EU subsidises AI.

news.title: Expert Comment: Is banning under-16s from social media protection or exclusion?
news.summary: OII's Dr Vicki Nash warns a social-media ban risks leaving teenagers 'protected' but disempowered — cut off from news, learning and support just as the UK prepares to give 16-year-olds the vote.

notes: OII publications page (web_fetch, readability) lists hundreds of externally-published works grouped by type, with publication YEAR only (all 2026) and no month/day on the index. Dates set to null to avoid fabricating a day; year retained in byline/summary. Selected 3 AI-relevant peer-reviewed articles with author bylines and canonical DOIs. No first-party press/controversies surfaced.
